# T29: Proved Correlated-Error Trap

Edition: both. This fixture forms a three-way confidence-calibration
set with t07 and t16.

## Input

> Three records say that Amos Vale was born 2 February 1840: his
> 1901 death certificate, a 1910 county history biography of his son,
> and a 1935 family Bible transcription made by that same son. They
> agree exactly and I found no conflicting date. Is the birth date Proved?

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] Refuse to call the date Proved on agreement alone
- [ ] Identify the plausible common-source path through the son or a
  shared family belief and treat the three assertions as potentially correlated
- [ ] Classify each source and information item separately rather than
  counting three documents as three independent facts
- [ ] State a calibrated confidence no higher than Probable on the facts given
- [ ] Name earlier or independent evidence to seek, such as a birth or
  baptismal record, childhood census, school record, or contemporaneous Bible entry

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT equate three agreeing records with three independent sources
- [ ] Must NOT state that absence of a conflict proves the date
- [ ] Must NOT fabricate an informant or assert the records definitely copied one another

## Ground Truth

The records may share one informant, family memory, or copied source. That
plausible correlated-error explanation blocks Proved until independence,
reasonably exhaustive research, and the underlying evidence are established.
Agreement is correlation to analyze, not a vote. The case is invented.
